issue_title: "Release requestToExecute memory when it is not needed by replication" introduces a fatal race condition
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

main

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

N/A

### Problem Description

https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/146247 introduces the potential for a race condition that can crash Elasticsearch. If, concurrently:

- Bulk operations are being performed on an index, and those bulk operations contain update operations
- A replica for the index is initializing

The replica can receive one or more of the update operations, hitting [this error handler](https://github.com/Mikep86/elasticsearch/blob/30f603e45847ceab6a5872f48843f1259a8b108a/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/action/bulk/TransportShardBulkAction.java#L855-L856).

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an index with one or more replicas
2. Before the index is in green state, execute bulk operations that contain updates on it

commit_message:
Fix field name length limit tests (#148161)

These tests were very rarely failing due to the dynamically-mapped field
field_name_not_ignored missing in the reported mappings.

I believe this was caused by a race condition, where the
indices.get_mapping call was rarely being executed by a node that had not
yet received the cluster state update with the updated mappings.

This test fixes this issue by waiting for all pending cluster state updates
to complete before calling indices.get_mapping.

commit_message:
ESQL: Re-land single-file schema caching with null-safe lastModified (#147390)

Re-applies the single-file schema caching from #147297 (reverted in
#147371) with a fix for the null lastModified NPE that caused the
revert.

StorageObject.lastModified() may return null per its SPI contract.
The original code called .toEpochMilli() without a null check,
causing NPEs for GCS, Azure, and gRPC/Flight providers. Guard the
direct call site in ExternalSourceResolver and normalize null to
Instant.EPOCH in StorageEntry so all downstream consumers are
protected.

Closes #147211

issue_title: [CI] `repeat-changed-tests` generates wrong Gradle project path for tests under `test/external-modules/`
issue_body:
# [CI] `repeat-changed-tests` generates wrong Gradle project path for tests under `test/external-modules/`

## Summary

`.buildkite/scripts/repeat-changed-tests.ts` infers the Gradle project path directly from a test file's on-disk directory. For any project under `test/external-modules/<name>/...`, this produces `:test:external-modules:<name>`, but the actual Gradle project is `:test:external-modules:test-<name>` because `settings.gradle` renames every child of `:test:external-modules` by prepending `test-`. As a result, whenever a PR changes a test file under `test/external-modules/*`, the generated `repeat-changed-tests` step fails with `Cannot locate tasks that match ':test:external-modules:<name>:...' as project '<name>' not found in project ':test:external-modules'`, and retries the same wrong command 10 times.

PR #147303 (`soft_fail`) prevents this from blocking merges, but the underlying bug remains and produces noisy red builds whenever `test/external-modules/*` is touched.

## Reproduction

Anywhere on the repo (no CI needed):

```shell
$ ./gradlew projects | grep apm-integration
|    |    +--- Project ':test:external-modules:test-apm-integration' - Apm integration plugin
project ':test:external-modules:test-apm-integration' - /test/external-modules/apm-integration

$ ./gradlew :test:external-modules:apm-integration:help         # path that the pipeline generates
FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.
* What went wrong:
Cannot locate tasks that match ':test:external-modules:apm-integration:help' as project 'apm-integration' not found in project ':test:external-modules'.

$ ./gradlew :test:external-modules:test-apm-integration:help    # correct path
BUILD SUCCESSFUL
```

Live example: https://buildkite.com/elastic/elasticsearch-pull-request/builds/139808#019dbab5-0bb4-4e10-931c-7a7fffb7168b (job "java rest tests", from PR #146095). The detect-changed-tests step logs the generated command:

```
command: .ci/scripts/repeat-rest-test.sh 10 .ci/scripts/run-gradle.sh
  :test:external-modules:apm-integration:javaRestTest
  --tests org.elasticsearch.test.apmintegration.AbstractTracesIT
  --tests org.elasticsearch.test.apmintegration.ApmAgentTracesIT --rerun
```

The task is then rejected by Gradle for all 10 iterations.

## Root cause

In `.buildkite/scripts/repeat-changed-tests.ts`:

```ts
// line 73
export function toGradleProject(path: string): string {
  return ":" + path.replace(/\//g, ":");
}
```

`path` comes from the regex captures in `SOURCE_SET_PATTERNS` (line 15), which extract the project-root directory (e.g. `test/external-modules/apm-integration`). `toGradleProject` assumes the directory path equals the Gradle project path, but `settings.gradle:173-175` renames every child of `:test:external-modules`:

```groovy
project(":test:external-modules").children.each { testProject ->
  testProject.name = "test-${testProject.name}"
}
```

So for any path that begins with `test/external-modules/<name>/`, the generated Gradle path is off by a missing `test-` prefix on the last segment.

## Scope of impact

Every project currently under `test/external-modules/` has test sources that the script would match:

| on-disk path | actual Gradle project | source sets present |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `test/external-modules/apm-integration` | `:test:external-modules:test-apm-integration` | `javaRestTest` |
| `test/external-modules/delayed-aggs` | `:test:external-modules:test-delayed-aggs` | `test`, `internalClusterTest`, `yamlRestTest` |
| `test/external-modules/die-with-dignity` | `:test:external-modules:test-die-with-dignity` | `javaRestTest` |
| `test/external-modules/error-query` | `:test:external-modules:test-error-query` | `test`, `javaRestTest`, `yamlRestTest` |
| `test/external-modules/esql-heap-attack` | `:test:external-modules:test-esql-heap-attack` | `javaRestTest` |
| `test/external-modules/jvm-crash` | `:test:external-modules:test-jvm-crash` | `javaRestTest` |
| `test/external-modules/latency-simulating-directory` | `:test:external-modules:test-latency-simulating-directory` | `internalClusterTest` |
| `test/external-modules/multi-project` | `:test:external-modules:test-multi-project` | `test`, `javaRestTest` |

Any PR that edits a test file in any of these directories will hit this bug. It is not specific to `apm-integration`.

## Suggested fix

The cleanest fix is to make `toGradleProject` aware of the `:test:external-modules` rename rule. A minimal patch in `.buildkite/scripts/repeat-changed-tests.ts`:

```ts
export function toGradleProject(path: string): string {
  const segments = path.split("/");
  // Mirror the rename in settings.gradle: children of :test:external-modules
  // have their project name prefixed with "test-".
  if (segments[0] === "test" && segments[1] === "external-modules" && segments.length >= 3) {
    segments[2] = `test-${segments[2]}`;
  }
  return ":" + segments.join(":");
}
```

Existing unit tests for `repeat-changed-tests.ts` should be extended so a change to a file under `test/external-modules/apm-integration/src/javaRestTest/...` produces a step whose command contains `:test:external-modules:test-apm-integration:javaRestTest`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Changing a test file in any `test/external-modules/<name>/` project produces a `repeat-changed-tests` step whose Gradle task path starts with `:test:external-modules:test-<name>:`.
- [ ] Unit tests in `.buildkite/scripts/pull-request/pipeline.test.ts` (or the `repeat-changed-tests.ts` tests, wherever appropriate) cover this case.
- [ ] A re-run on a PR that touches `test/external-modules/apm-integration` (for example PR #146095) no longer produces "project not found" failures on the `repeat-changed-tests:java-rest` step.
commit_message:
Fix repeat-changed-tests Gradle path for test/external-modules (#147340)

toGradleProject in .buildkite/scripts/repeat-changed-tests.ts derived 
the Gradle project path from the on-disk
directory, but settings.gradle renames every direct child of
:test:external-modules by prepending "test-". As a result, any PR that
changed a test under test/external-modules/<name>/ generated a step
targeting :test:external-modules:<name>:..., which Gradle rejects with
"project not found" and retries 10 times.

Mirror the rename rule in toGradleProject and extend the unit tests.

Fixes #147332

issue_body:
Introduced in: 7b77621e7a389a02a4d50b9ebf00f145b006612d

Steps to reproduce:
```
 ./gradlew ":qa:mixed-cluster:v9.4.0#mixedClusterTest" -Dtests.class="org.elasticsearch.backwards.MixedClusterClientYamlTestSuiteIT" -Dtests.method="test {p0=aggregations/terms_text_docvalues/Indexing disabled}" -Dtests.seed=C9D785607134B6FC -Dtests.bwc=true -Druntime.java=26
```

In https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/commit/7b77621e7a389a02a4d50b9ebf00f145b006612d, we introduced [DocValuesFieldFactory](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/cf893691091dd651607f0dee109e07f27818285d/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/index/mapper/DocValuesFieldFactory.java#L30), which is meant to act as the one place responsible for creating doc values fields.

This factory contains an `IndexVersion`, which is passed down to `MultiValuedBinaryDocValuesField` and used it to decide what binary doc values encoding format to use (`IntegratedCounts` or `SeparateCounts`). This is important for BWC purpose - where some callers previously used `IntegratedCounts`, but now use `SeparateCounts`.

This BWC check however is unnecessary for callers that always used `SeparateCounts`. Branching on the index version for such callers is wrong as it forces them to use an encoding format they never supported. This results in NPEs in `AbstractBinaryDocValuesQuery` as it only understands the `SeparateCounts` format.

Related issue: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/147200

issue_title: Running the full ES|QL test suite deletes all Kibana command definition files
issue_body:
Running:
```
./gradlew ":x-pack:plugin:esql:test"
```

Deletes all the JSON files in docs/reference/query-languages/esql/kibana/definition/commands/. These are used by Kibana to control its understanding of ES|QL commands.
commit_message:
Take more care not to delete kibana definition files (#147419)

When, for example, one test is muted which would stop some subdirectory from being created, this should not trigger deletions.

commit_message:
Fix field name length limit tests (#148161)

These tests were very rarely failing due to the dynamically-mapped field
field_name_not_ignored missing in the reported mappings.

I believe this was caused by a race condition, where the
indices.get_mapping call was rarely being executed by a node that had not
yet received the cluster state update with the updated mappings.

This test fixes this issue by waiting for all pending cluster state updates
to complete before calling indices.get_mapping.

[Transform] fix randomInvalidScriptConfig (#148357)

randomInvalidScriptConfig was generating a random valid config with the
field `id`. Removing all valid script field names from the random
invalid generation.

Resolve #147543
